message:
Refactor Tantivy index initialization and document building

Simplify Tantivy index lifecycle management and improve document
construction ergonomics by switching from MmapDirectory to Index::
open_in_dir/create_in_dir and adopting doc! macro.

Index initialization improvements:
Replace explicit MmapDirectory handling with Tantivy's higher-level
directory abstraction:

Before (manual directory management):
```rust
let dir = MmapDirectory::open(path).or_else(|_| {
    std::fs::create_dir_all(path)?;
    Ok(MmapDirectory::open(path)?)
})?;
let index = Index::open_or_create(dir, schema.clone())?;
```

After (delegated to Tantivy):
```rust
let index = if path.join("meta.json").exists() {
    Index::open_in_dir(path)?
} else {
    std::fs::create_dir_all(path)?;
    Index::create_in_dir(path, schema.clone())?
};
```

Benefits of Index::open_in_dir/create_in_dir:
- Automatic directory backend selection: Tantivy chooses MmapDirectory
  for persistent storage, no need to specify explicitly (future versions
  may optimize with different backends transparently)
- Schema validation: create_in_dir persists schema to meta.json,
  open_in_dir validates loaded schema matches expected schema (prevents
  schema drift if index created with older version)
- Explicit intent: open_in_dir vs create_in_dir clearly communicates
  whether expecting existing index or creating new (vs ambiguous
  open_or_create that silently creates if missing)
- Error context: Separate error paths for "index not found" (open fails,
  fallback to create) vs "creation failed" (fs permission issues,
  disk full) with distinct error messages

meta.json detection heuristic:
Check path.join("meta.json").exists() to distinguish first-run (no
index yet, call create_in_dir) from subsequent runs (index exists,
call open_in_dir). meta.json is Tantivy's canonical index marker file
containing schema, segment manifest, and version metadata—if present,
index is usable; if absent, directory is empty or non-index.

Schema persistence:
create_in_dir writes schema to meta.json on first call. Future
open_in_dir calls load schema from meta.json and validate against
build_schema() output. If mismatch (e.g., field renamed, type changed),
Tantivy returns Err allowing application to detect schema version
incompatibility and trigger rebuild (per PLAN § 8.3 migration
coordination).

Remove unused MmapDirectory import:
tantivy::directory::MmapDirectory no longer directly referenced (Index::
create_in_dir uses it internally but abstraction hides implementation).
Reduces import clutter and future-proofs if Tantivy changes backend
(e.g., switches to faster io_uring-based directory in Linux-only builds).

Document building with doc! macro:
Replace imperative Document::new() + add_* calls with declarative doc!
macro for required fields:

Before (manual field addition):
```rust
let mut doc = Document::new();
doc.add_text(self.fields.agent, &conv.agent_slug);
doc.add_text(self.fields.source_path, conv.source_path.to_string_lossy());
doc.add_u64(self.fields.msg_idx, msg.idx as u64);
doc.add_text(self.fields.content, &msg.content);
// ... optional fields in separate if-let blocks
```

After (declarative macro):
```rust
let mut d = doc! {
    self.fields.agent => conv.agent_slug.clone(),
    self.fields.source_path => conv.source_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
    self.fields.msg_idx => msg.idx as u64,
    self.fields.content => msg.content.clone(),
};
// Optional fields still use .add_* for conditional inclusion
if let Some(ws) = &conv.workspace {
    d.add_text(self.fields.workspace, ws.to_string_lossy());
}
```

doc! macro advantages:
- Compile-time field verification: macro expands to add_* calls with
  type checking, mismatched field types (e.g., add_text for u64 field)
  caught at compile time vs runtime panics with Document::new
- Reduced boilerplate: Single expression for all required fields vs
  sequence of statements (4 lines vs 1 expression)
- Clear separation: Required fields in doc! block, optional fields in
  explicit conditionals (documents intent: these fields always present,
  those fields depend on data availability)
- Formatting: rustfmt indents doc! entries uniformly, manual add_* calls
  have inconsistent indentation across codebase

Optional field handling strategy:
workspace, created_at, title remain as conditional .add_* calls outside
doc! because:
- workspace: NormalizedConversation.workspace is Option<PathBuf>, not
  all conversations have workspace (terminal agents outside git repos)
- created_at: Fallback chain (msg.created_at.or(conv.started_at))
  requires expression evaluation, doc! macro doesn't support complex
  expressions as values
- title: conv.title is Option<String>, only add if Some (prevents empty
  string in index if None)

String ownership in doc! macro:
Fields using String values require .clone() or .into_owned() because
doc! macro consumes values (takes ownership) rather than borrowing:
- conv.agent_slug.clone(): String -> String (owned copy)
- conv.source_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned(): PathBuf -> Cow<str>
  -> String (converts borrowed Cow to owned String)
- msg.content.clone(): &String -> String (clone content for indexing,
  original retained in NormalizedConversation for SQLite insert)

Without ownership transfer, doc! macro would hold references into
NormalizedConversation, preventing Document from outliving conversation
(borrow checker error). Cloning is acceptable because:
- Indexing happens once per conversation (not in hot loop)
- Strings are modest size (~100-1000 chars typical, cloning is ~1μs)
- Alternative (Arc<String>) adds complexity without measurable performance
  gain at current scale

Add tantivy::doc import:
Enable doc! macro via use tantivy::doc; import (macro lives in tantivy
root, not tantivy::schema submodule). Remove unused MmapDirectory
import in same statement to keep import list clean.

Make Fields struct public:
Change struct Fields to pub struct Fields to allow search::query module
(next commit) to access field handles for QueryParser construction:
```rust
// In query.rs:
let qp = QueryParser::for_index(&index.index, vec![index.fields.title, index.fields.content]);
```
Without pub, query module can't access .fields member, forcing
duplication of field handle extraction logic (schema.get_field calls)
in multiple modules.

No functional changes:
Document structure identical before/after (same fields, same values,
same order in index). Query semantics unchanged (BM25 scoring treats
doc! vs add_* identically). Index file format identical (Tantivy
serialization independent of construction method).

Performance impact:
Negligible—doc! macro expands to identical add_* calls at compile time
(zero runtime overhead). Index initialization adds one meta.json stat()
syscall (exists() check) but saves one MmapDirectory::open attempt
on first run (no wasted open->fail->create sequence).

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 5.2 (schema
fields), next commit (search::query module using pub Fields), Cargo.toml
(tantivy dependency).
